## [00:00 - 03:02]

Welcome to this session. Obviously by now you know who I am and I know who you are. You are a deserving man or woman who is at a point in your life, either the beginning or middle or in who has decided that you are ready to create and build a business, an enterprise, a company of your own that can be successful in either Japan or in all kinds of other parts of the world. You've gone through the modules and you see that there are a lot of options, a lot of opportunities, a lot of different ways you can get started, a lot of ways you can do it with no money and very little or no risk. So today we're going to visit a concept that is universal. No matter what business you go into, no matter what level of size you are, whether you're a one man or woman business or whether you start a team or whether you start by joint venturing with other salespeople or organizations, you need to master the understanding and the belief in the importance and the meaning of what is called consultative or advisory selling. Let me start to explain and illustrate. All elements of life and all elements of business require and incorporate selling. You sell obviously to prospects, your products or services. You sell to distributors the ability to take on your products or services. You sell to prospective employees the vision you have and the value of coming to work for you and really dedicating their career to you. You as a manager have to sell your team on your vision and on helping achieve it in its fullest dimension. Most people worldwide, not just in Japan, but worldwide believe that selling is manipulative, that it's a bunch of techniques, it's a bunch of scripts, it's a bunch of psychological

## [03:03 - 06:10]

manipulation that is so far from the truth that it is almost laughable. To be massively and continually successful in whatever business you start or buy or partner with, you need to master consultative advisory selling. It is totally, it is stunningly, it is impressively different than every other form of selling. Let me differentiate. Most people who try to teach their salespeople how to sell don't even teach them how to sell. They just teach them their product line or the characteristics of the product. The poor salesperson goes out in the market and sells just on knowledge, not on understanding human nature or understanding psychology or understanding the needs of a buyer or prospective buyer. Advisory selling is the ultimate and exquisite way to own not just a marketplace, but to be beloved and outrageously effective in all your management leadership and financial selling activities. Now what is it? It is a strategic way of thinking that allows you to represent to the prospects you try to sell to or the team you recruit or the managers that lead the fact that you are in fact a leader and your role is to be the ultimate advisor, the ultimate consultant, to give advice, to give perspective, to give truth, to help evaluate situations to each category. For example, if you are trying to sell a prospective direct buyer and you are using consultative selling, the first thing you need to do is help the buyer develop a clear-cut understanding of what outcome, of what result, of what benefit, of what goal, of what negative they are trying to avoid and what positive they are trying to achieve. That is accomplished by serving as their advisor, by guiding them, by questioning them through

## [06:10 - 09:11]

socratic interviews, socratic interviews asking meaningful questions that allow them to discover within themselves answers. Then a consultative or advisory salesperson takes those answers and gives well-reasoned, totally objective and externally focused advice and recommendations. By that I mean you are not focused on just selling anybody anything you can for any reason. You are committed and you are totally focused on advising everybody in what is then their best interest and why. You give them reality as you see it. You tell them what you recommend they do, why you recommend they do it, what you believe the outcome of doing it will be for them, what the penalty of not doing it will be and you respectfully and not disparagingly establish why doing it with and through your business is a wiser decision than doing it through a competitor or an alternative or not doing it at all. It is taking on the role of literally a professional advisor or consultant and that is a very powerful position to take because you are always looking out at the best interest and for the best interest in the prospect or client. As we discussed in preeminence and there is a very big integration and overlap between the two, client means many things in your business. There are like four categories of clients. The first clients are the ones who pay you, prospects and buyers. The second category of clients are the ones you pay, your team, your vendors, your advisors. You need to serve as their advisor and their consultant as well. If somebody has value and talent they can choose working for anyone. If you are a small beginning business you probably don't have the deepest pockets to pay the most money. You have to win talent on their ability to see your commitment to their growth, to their success, to their best interest. You have to guide them in ways and direct them in ways and advise them in ways that will make them the most powerful, the most impactful and the most successful for you, to and for the client and ultimately for themselves.

## [09:11 - 12:14]

If you are leading which any entrepreneur in business is a leader, you are on a crusade and the crusade is the better outcome for your clients. The best result, the best benefit, the best success, the best experience depends on what you sell and take your clients away from negatives. But you have to learn what being an advisor looks like, is experience like and what your mindset has to be. So your mindset is you are no longer trying to manipulate anyone for any reason. Manipulation is not allowed in consultative advisory preeminent selling. The key to it is advising, counseling, directing and consulting someone in what you believe in your heart of hearts is in their best interest. Any consultative selling like preeminence does not allow you to try to get people to buy what they should not buy. Conversely, it does not allow you to get them to buy less than they can buy. It does not allow you to get let them buy less quality or combinations than they can buy. It puts you in a role where you have a moral responsibility to the client to advise and tell them what is in their best interest and why and to project them forward to what it will be like when they take the action you recommend and how much better their life or their business or their body or their finances depending on what you are selling will be. A consultative selling is a universal trait, power, force and attribute every entrepreneur needs to master and needs to be able to teach, instill and install in all of your team. Why? Because when somebody calls on the phone and gets your receptionist, he or she has to be consultative to guide them in an authoritative way. When somebody calls your technical support or your customer service, they need to be consultative and advisory and authoritative in the same way. When someone delivers products, they need to be consultative and authoritative in the same way. When you recruit staff, you have to counsel and advise them in what is their best interest. If you don't believe that a certain person should not work in your company because you

do not give them the most successful environment, you have to tell them that. Consultative selling means enormous collaboration, cooperation and connection to your vendors.
